raw_consumer_statement:
I am a New York consumer disputing a financed credit card charge related to a vacation club membership sold under material misrepresentation. 

The merchant, XXXX XXXX XXXX, sold the membership during a high-pressure sales presentation while I was traveling. The salesperson promised unlimited discounted travel and guaranteed savings, which are not reflected in the written contract. The agreement is a long-term obligation with significant undisclosed limitations. 

After the initial purchase, the merchant contacted me again on XX/XX/year>, and sold an additional XXXX membership claiming it would reduce annual fees and improve benefits. This post-sale offer directly contradicts the original agreement and demonstrates deceptive sales practices. 

I sent written notice of cancellation, which the merchant acknowledged. I have not used any services. I disputed the charges with Barclays. Barclays completed an investigation ( Case # XXXX ) and denied permanent credit, relying primarily on the existence of a contract and not adequately considering evidence of misrepresentation, post-sale manipulation, and regulatory complaints. 

I have filed complaints with the Federal Trade Commission ( Report # XXXX ), the XXXX XXXX XXXX ( complaint closed unresolved ), and the New York Attorney General ( Intake # XXXX ). 

I am requesting executive-level review of Barclays handling of this dispute, confirmation that payments and interest remain frozen during review, assurance that no negative credit reporting occurs, and reconsideration of the disputed charge based on deceptive business practices.
